key_insight: A two-level Schwarz coarse solver with a structured, non-nested global coarse space can replace AMG as the coarse-level solver in p-multigrid for Poisson problems.
engineering_relevance: This offers a scalable alternative for pressure Poisson solves in high-order incompressible CFD, targeting exascale performance on modern supercomputers.
method: Local solve in the original p-multigrid coarse space
Global coarse problem built from a novel structured non-nested coarse space
Communication-free interpolation between local and global levels
evidence_signal: The method is evaluated against BoomerAMG using Nek5000/RS on Summit and Frontier, showing performance through a series of scalability experiments.
watch_item: The structured coarse space may require careful implementation and integration with the existing p-multigrid hierarchy to preserve efficiency and robustness.
paper_title: CoarseSolvers for Exascale Solution of Poisson Problems
